scenes.text: In this lecture on Docker, we will explore the core concepts and architecture of Docker, an essential tool for modern software development and deployment. Docker is a platform designed to facilitate the creation, deployment, and execution of applications using containers. Containers provide a lightweight and efficient alternative to traditional virtual machines, allowing developers to package an application with all its dependencies into a standardized unit for software development. The lecture will cover how Docker streamlines the development process, enhances collaboration, and simplifies the deployment of applications across different environments.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A(Docker Host) --> B(Docker Engine); B --> C{Docker Daemon}; C --> D[Container 1]; C --> E[Container 2]; C --> F[Container 3]; G(Docker CLI) --> C; C --> H{Docker API}; H --> I[Docker Hub]
